#!/usr/bin/env bash
# Sweep stale MCP helper processes and temporary dispatch artifacts.
#
# Usage:
#   orphan-mcp-sweeper.sh [--dry-run] [--verbose] [--log-path PATH]
#
# Defaults:
#   ORPHAN_AGE_MIN_SEC=300
#   ORPHAN_TMP_AGE_HOURS=24
#   ORPHAN_SWEEPER_LOG_MAX_BYTES=10485760
#   ORPHAN_SWEEPER_LOG_PATH=$HOME/.local/share/orphan-sweeper.log

set -euo pipefail

# ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
# 1. RUNTIME CONFIGURATION
# ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

DRY_RUN=false
VERBOSE=false

display.text:
#!/usr/bin/env bash
# Sweep stale MCP helper processes and temporary dispatch artifacts.
#
# Usage:
#   orphan-mcp-sweeper.sh [--dry-run] [--verbose] [--log-path PATH]
#
# Defaults:
#   ORPHAN_AGE_MIN_SEC=300
#   ORPHAN_TMP_AGE_HOURS=24
#   ORPHAN_SWEEPER_LOG_MAX_BYTES=10485760
#   ORPHAN_SWEEPER_LOG_PATH=$HOME/.local/share/orphan-sweeper.log

set -euo pipefail

# ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
# 1. RUNTIME CONFIGURATION
# ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

DRY_RUN=false
VERBOSE=false
AGE_MIN_SEC="${ORPHAN_AGE_MIN_SEC:-300}"
TMP_AGE_HOURS="${ORPHAN_TMP_AGE_HOURS:-24}"
LOG_PATH="${ORPHAN_SWEEPER_LOG_PATH:-${HOME:-/tmp}/.local/share/orphan-sweeper.log}"
LOG_EXPLICIT=false
LOG_MAX_BYTES="${ORPHAN_SWEEPER_LOG_MAX_BYTES:-10485760}"
EXIT_CODE=0

CANDIDATE_PIDS=()
CANDIDATE_PPIDS=()
CANDIDATE_CLASSES=()
CANDIDATE_AGES=()
CANDIDATE_ETIMES=()
CANDIDATE_RSSES=()
CANDIDATE_COMMANDS=()

KILL_PIDS=()
KILL_CLASSES=()
KILL_AGES=()
KILL_ETIMES=()
KILL_RSSES=()
KILL_COMMANDS=()

MIN_CLASSES=()
MIN_AGES=()
MIN_PIDS=()

SESSION_TREE_PIDS=()

# ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
# 2. CLI PARSING
# ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

usage() {
  cat <<'EOF'
Usage: orphan-mcp-sweeper.sh [--dry-run] [--verbose] [--log-path PATH]

Options:
  --dry-run        Log matching kill/remove actions without mutating anything.
  --verbose        Log preserve decisions as well as actions.
  --log-path PATH  Append logs to PATH. Default: ~/.local/share/orphan-sweeper.log.
  -h, --help       Show this help.
EOF
}

while [[ "$#" -gt 0 ]]; do
  case "$1" in
    --dry-run)
      DRY_RUN=true
      ;;
    --verbose)
      VERBOSE=true
      ;;
    --log-path)
      if [[ "$#" -lt 2 ]]; then
        echo "ERROR: --log-path requires a path" >&2
        exit 1
      fi
      LOG_PATH="$2"
      LOG_EXPLICIT=true
      shift
      ;;
    -h|--help)
      usage
      exit 0
      ;;
    *)
      echo "ERROR: unknown argument: $1" >&2
      usage >&2
      exit 1
      ;;
  esac
  shift
done

# ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
# 3. VALIDATION HELPERS
# ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

is_positive_int() {
  case "$1" in
    ''|*[!0-9]*) return 1 ;;
    *) [[ "$1" -gt 0 ]] ;;
  esac
}

if ! is_positive_int "$AGE_MIN_SEC"; then
  AGE_MIN_SEC=300
fi
if ! is_positive_int "$TMP_AGE_HOURS"; then
  TMP_AGE_HOURS=24
fi
if ! is_positive_int "$LOG_MAX_BYTES"; then
  LOG_MAX_BYTES=10485760
fi

should_append_log_file() {
  [[ "$LOG_EXPLICIT" = true ]] || [[ -t 1 ]]
}

# ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
# 4. LOGGING
# ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

rotate_log_if_needed() {
  should_append_log_file || return 0
  [[ -n "$LOG_PATH" ]] || return 0
  [[ -f "$LOG_PATH" ]] || return 0

  local size
  size="$(wc -c < "$LOG_PATH" 2>/dev/null || echo 0)"
  is_positive_int "$size" || size=0
  [[ "$size" -le "$LOG_MAX_BYTES" ]] && return 0

  mv -f "$LOG_PATH.2" "$LOG_PATH.3" 2>/dev/null || true
  mv -f "$LOG_PATH.1" "$LOG_PATH.2" 2>/dev/null || true
  mv -f "$LOG_PATH" "$LOG_PATH.1" 2>/dev/null || true
}

emit() {
  local line
  line="$(date '+%Y-%m-%dT%H:%M:%S%z') $*"
  printf '%s\n' "$line"
  if should_append_log_file && [[ -n "$LOG_PATH" ]]; then
    mkdir -p "$(dirname "$LOG_PATH")" 2>/dev/null || true
    printf '%s\n' "$line" >> "$LOG_PATH" 2>/dev/null || true
  fi
}

log_action() {
  if [[ "$DRY_RUN" = true ]]; then
    emit "[DRY-RUN] $*"
  else
    emit "$*"
  fi
}

log_verbose() {
  [[ "$VERBOSE" = true ]] || return 0
  emit "$*"
}

# ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
# 5. PROCESS CLASSIFICATION
# ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

etime_to_seconds() {
  local raw="$1"
  local days=0
  local rest="$raw"
  local h=0
  local m=0
  local s=0
  local a b c

  case "$rest" in
    *-*)
      days="${rest%%-*}"
      rest="${rest#*-}"
      ;;
  esac

  IFS=: read -r a b c <<EOF
$rest
EOF
  if [[ -n "${c:-}" ]]; then
    h="$a"
    m="$b"
    s="$c"
  elif [[ -n "${b:-}" ]]; then
    m="$a"
    s="$b"
  else
    s="$a"
  fi

  days="${days#0}"; h="${h#0}"; m="${m#0}"; s="${s#0}"
  days="${days:-0}"; h="${h:-0}"; m="${m:-0}"; s="${s:-0}"
  printf '%s\n' $((days * 86400 + h * 3600 + m * 60 + s))
}

classify_command() {
  local cmd="$1"
  case "$cmd" in
    *"mk-spec-memory-launcher.cjs"*) printf '%s\n' "mk-spec-memory-launcher"; return 0 ;;
    *"mk-skill-advisor-launcher.cjs"*) printf '%s\n' "mk-skill-advisor-launcher"; return 0 ;;
    *"mk-code-index-launcher.cjs"*) printf '%s\n' "mk-code-index-launcher"; return 0 ;;
    *"system-spec-kit/mcp_server/dist/context-server.js"*) printf '%s\n' "spec-memory-context-server"; return 0 ;;
    *"system-skill-advisor/mcp_server/dist/"*"advisor-server.js"*) printf '%s\n' "skill-advisor-server"; return 0 ;;
    *"system-code-graph/mcp_server/dist/index.js"*) printf '%s\n' "code-graph-server"; return 0 ;;
    *"mcp-code-mode/mcp_server/dist/index.js"*) printf '%s\n' "mcp-code-mode"; return 0 ;;
    *"npm exec @taazkareem/clickup-mcp-server"*) printf '%s\n' "clickup-mcp-wrapper"; return 0 ;;
    *"clickup-mcp-server"*) printf '%s\n' "clickup-mcp-server"; return 0 ;;
    *"npm exec @modelcontextprotocol/server-sequential-thinking"*)
      printf '%s\n' "sequential-thinking-wrapper"
      return 0
      ;;
    *"@modelcontextprotocol/server-sequential-thinking"*|*"server-sequential-thinking"*)
      printf '%s\n' "sequential-thinking-server"
      return 0
      ;;
  esac
  return 1
}

class_index() {
  local class="$1"
  local i
  for i in "${!MIN_CLASSES[@]}"; do
    if [[ "${MIN_CLASSES[$i]}" = "$class" ]]; then
      printf '%s\n' "$i"
      return 0
    fi
  done
  return 1
}

remember_min_for_class() {
  local class="$1"
  local age="$2"
  local pid="$3"
  local idx
  idx="$(class_index "$class" 2>/dev/null || true)"
  if [[ -z "$idx" ]]; then
    MIN_CLASSES+=("$class")
    MIN_AGES+=("$age")
    MIN_PIDS+=("$pid")
    return
  fi
  if (( age < MIN_AGES[idx] )); then
    MIN_AGES[$idx]="$age"
    MIN_PIDS[$idx]="$pid"
  fi
}

freshest_pid_for_class() {
  local class="$1"
  local idx
  idx="$(class_index "$class" 2>/dev/null || true)"
  [[ -n "$idx" ]] || return 1
  printf '%s\n' "${MIN_PIDS[$idx]}"
}

freshest_age_for_class() {
  local class="$1"
  local idx
  idx="$(class_index "$class" 2>/dev/null || true)"
  [[ -n "$idx" ]] || return 1
  printf '%s\n' "${MIN_AGES[$idx]}"
}

pid_in_list() {
  local needle="$1"
  shift
  local item
  for item in "$@"; do
    [[ "$item" = "$needle" ]] && return 0
  done
  return 1
}

# ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
# 6. SESSION TREE DISCOVERY
# ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

append_descendants() {
  local parent="$1"
  local child
  while read -r child; do
    [[ -n "$child" ]] || continue
    if [[ "${#SESSION_TREE_PIDS[@]}" -eq 0 ]] || ! pid_in_list "$child" "${SESSION_TREE_PIDS[@]}"; then
      SESSION_TREE_PIDS+=("$child")
      append_descendants "$child"
    fi
  done <<EOF
$(pgrep -P "$parent" 2>/dev/null || true)
EOF
}

# Build the live operator-session process tree across ALL AI CLI runtimes, not just
# Claude Code. Any MCP helper descended from a live claude / opencode / codex
# session belongs to an active operator session and must be preserved — most importantly
# an operator's `opencode run`, which previously fell outside the Claude-only tree and
# got swept after ORPHAN_AGE_MIN_SEC (a hard-rule violation).
build_session_trees() {
  local pid
  while read -r pid; do
    [[ -n "$pid" ]] || continue
    if [[ "${#SESSION_TREE_PIDS[@]}" -eq 0 ]] || ! pid_in_list "$pid" "${SESSION_TREE_PIDS[@]}"; then
      SESSION_TREE_PIDS+=("$pid")
      append_descendants "$pid"
    fi
  done <<EOF
$(pgrep -f '(^|/)(claude|opencode|codex)( |$)|Claude Code' 2>/dev/null || true)
EOF
}

has_non_mcp_listener() {
  local pid="$1"
  local line
  while read -r line; do
    case "$line" in
      *LISTEN*)
        return 0
        ;;
    esac
  done <<EOF
$(lsof -nP -a -p "$pid" -iTCP -sTCP:LISTEN 2>/dev/null || true)
EOF
  return 1
}

# The MCP daemons bridge sibling sessions over a UNIX socket (daemon-ipc.sock),
# NOT TCP, so has_non_mcp_listener never covers them. Under default-on daemon
# re-election a still-serving daemon is reparented to pid 1 and falls outside every
# live-session tree — yet killing it drops the transport for any concurrent live
# session bridged to it. A daemon actively serving connections holds MORE THAN ONE
# unix-socket FD on its daemon-ipc.sock (the listener + one per live peer); a bare
# listener with no clients holds exactly one. Treat >1 as "in use" and preserve it.
has_live_ipc_socket_connection() {
  local pid="$1"
  local count
  count="$(lsof -nP -a -p "$pid" -U 2>/dev/null | grep -c 'daemon-ipc\.sock' || true)"
  [[ -n "$count" ]] || count=0
  [[ "$count" -gt 1 ]] 2>/dev/null
}

# ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
# 7. PRESERVATION POLICY
# ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

preserve_reason() {
  local pid="$1"
  local class="$2"
  local age="$3"
  local cmd="$4"
  local freshest_pid
  local freshest_age

  # Operator-owned interactive/dispatch sessions across runtimes. These commands name a
  # session the operator is actively running; their MCP helpers must never be swept.
  # build_session_trees covers descendants via process ancestry; these patterns also
  # catch the session's own root process whatever its descendants look like.
  case "$cmd" in
    *"opencode run"*) printf '%s\n' "operator-opencode-preserve"; return 0 ;;
    *"codex exec"*) printf '%s\n' "operator-codex-preserve"; return 0 ;;
    *"ollama runner"*|*"ollama serve"*) printf '%s\n' "ollama-preserve"; return 0 ;;
  esac

  if [[ "${#SESSION_TREE_PIDS[@]}" -gt 0 ]] && pid_in_list "$pid" "${SESSION_TREE_PIDS[@]}"; then
    printf '%s\n' "live-session-tree"
    return 0
  fi

  freshest_pid="$(freshest_pid_for_class "$class" 2>/dev/null || true)"
  freshest_age="$(freshest_age_for_class "$class" 2>/dev/null || echo 999999)"
  if [[ "$pid" = "$freshest_pid" ]] && [[ "$freshest_age" -lt "$AGE_MIN_SEC" ]]; then
    printf '%s\n' "freshest-young-instance"
    return 0
  fi

  if has_non_mcp_listener "$pid"; then
    printf '%s\n' "active-non-mcp-tcp-listener"
    return 0
  fi

  if has_live_ipc_socket_connection "$pid"; then
    printf '%s\n' "active-ipc-socket-connection"
    return 0
  fi

  return 1
}

# ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
# 8. PROCESS SCANNING
# ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

scan_processes() {
  local pid _ppid etime rss _stat command class age
  while read -r pid _ppid etime rss _stat command; do
    [[ -n "${pid:-}" ]] || continue
    class="$(classify_command "${command:-}" 2>/dev/null || true)"
    [[ -n "$class" ]] || continue
    age="$(etime_to_seconds "$etime")"
    CANDIDATE_PIDS+=("$pid")
    CANDIDATE_PPIDS+=("$_ppid")
    CANDIDATE_CLASSES+=("$class")
    CANDIDATE_AGES+=("$age")
    CANDIDATE_ETIMES+=("$etime")
    CANDIDATE_RSSES+=("$rss")
    CANDIDATE_COMMANDS+=("${command:-}")
    remember_min_for_class "$class" "$age" "$pid"
  done <<EOF
$(ps -axo pid=,ppid=,etime=,rss=,stat=,command=)
EOF
}

select_kill_candidates() {
  local i pid class age etime rss cmd reason
  for i in "${!CANDIDATE_PIDS[@]}"; do
    pid="${CANDIDATE_PIDS[$i]}"
    : "${CANDIDATE_PPIDS[$i]}"
    class="${CANDIDATE_CLASSES[$i]}"
    age="${CANDIDATE_AGES[$i]}"
    etime="${CANDIDATE_ETIMES[$i]}"
    rss="${CANDIDATE_RSSES[$i]}"
    cmd="${CANDIDATE_COMMANDS[$i]}"

    reason="$(preserve_reason "$pid" "$class" "$age" "$cmd" 2>/dev/null || true)"
    if [[ -n "$reason" ]]; then
      log_verbose "action=preserve reason=$reason pid=$pid class=$class rss_kb=$rss age_sec=$age etime=$etime command=$cmd"
      continue
    fi

    KILL_PIDS+=("$pid")
    KILL_CLASSES+=("$class")
    KILL_AGES+=("$age")
    KILL_ETIMES+=("$etime")
    KILL_RSSES+=("$rss")
    KILL_COMMANDS+=("$cmd")
  done
}

# ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
# 9. TERMINATION
# ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

terminate_candidates() {
  local i pid class age etime rss cmd
  for i in "${!KILL_PIDS[@]}"; do
    pid="${KILL_PIDS[$i]}"
    class="${KILL_CLASSES[$i]}"
    age="${KILL_AGES[$i]}"
    etime="${KILL_ETIMES[$i]}"
    rss="${KILL_RSSES[$i]}"
    cmd="${KILL_COMMANDS[$i]}"

    log_action "action=kill signal=TERM pid=$pid class=$class rss_kb=$rss age_sec=$age etime=$etime command=$cmd"
    if [[ "$DRY_RUN" = false ]]; then
      if ! kill -15 "$pid" 2>/dev/null; then
        emit "action=kill-failed signal=TERM pid=$pid class=$class"
        EXIT_CODE=1
      fi
    fi
  done

  [[ "$DRY_RUN" = true ]] && return 0
  [[ "${#KILL_PIDS[@]}" -eq 0 ]] && return 0

  sleep 5
  for i in "${!KILL_PIDS[@]}"; do
    pid="${KILL_PIDS[$i]}"
    class="${KILL_CLASSES[$i]}"
    if kill -0 "$pid" 2>/dev/null; then
      emit "action=kill signal=KILL pid=$pid class=$class"
      if ! kill -9 "$pid" 2>/dev/null; then
        emit "action=kill-failed signal=KILL pid=$pid class=$class"
        EXIT_CODE=1
      fi
    fi
  done
}

# ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
# 10. TEMPORARY ARTIFACT SWEEPING
# ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

path_mtime_epoch() {
  local path="$1"
  stat -f %m "$path" 2>/dev/null || stat -c %Y "$path" 2>/dev/null || printf '%s\n' 0
}

path_is_old_enough() {
  local path="$1"
  local now_epoch mtime age min_age
  now_epoch="$(date +%s)"
  mtime="$(path_mtime_epoch "$path")"
  is_positive_int "$mtime" || return 1
  age=$((now_epoch - mtime))
  min_age=$((TMP_AGE_HOURS * 3600))
  [[ "$age" -ge "$min_age" ]]
}

preserve_tmp_path() {
  local path="$1"
  local base="${path##*/}"
  case "$base" in
    codex-browser-use|*cache*|*Cache*) return 0 ;;
  esac
  return 1
}

remove_tmp_path() {
  local path="$1"
  [[ -e "$path" ]] || return 0
  preserve_tmp_path "$path" && { log_verbose "action=preserve-tmp path=$path"; return 0; }
  path_is_old_enough "$path" || { log_verbose "action=preserve-tmp reason=too-new path=$path"; return 0; }

  log_action "action=remove-tmp path=$path"
  if [[ "$DRY_RUN" = false ]]; then
    if ! rm -rf "$path" 2>/dev/null; then
      emit "action=remove-tmp-failed path=$path"
      EXIT_CODE=1
    fi
  fi
}

sweep_tmp() {
  local path
  shopt -s nullglob
  for path in /tmp/codex-* /tmp/cli-* /tmp/opencode-* /tmp/deep-review-*; do
    [[ -d "$path" ]] || continue
    remove_tmp_path "$path"
  done
  for path in /tmp/save-context-data-* /tmp/*-prompt.md /tmp/*-cli-err.log /tmp/*-cli-out.log; do
    [[ -f "$path" ]] || continue
    remove_tmp_path "$path"
  done
  shopt -u nullglob
}

14:   exit 0
15: fi

109:   if ! node "$OWNERSHIP_LINT" >/dev/null; then
110:     echo "" >&2
111:     echo "BLOCKED: tool-ownership drift detected." >&2
112:     echo "Fix: regenerate mcp_server/tests/fixtures/tool-ownership-map.json from TOOL_DEFINITIONS." >&2
113:     echo "Bypass: SPECKIT_SKIP_TOOL_OWNERSHIP_LINT=1 git commit ..." >&2
114:     exit 1
115:   fi
116: fi
117: 
118: exit 0

(End of file - total 118 lines)

#!/usr/bin/env bash
# Pre-commit hook — advisory model-reference drift check.
#
# Runs validate-doc-model-refs.js on the working tree before each commit.
# Advisory mode: prints warnings on drift but does NOT block the commit.
# Bypass: SPECKIT_SKIP_DOC_MODEL_VALIDATE=1 git commit ...
#
# Install: bash .opencode/scripts/install-git-hooks.sh
# Uninstall: rm .git/hooks/pre-commit

set -euo pipefail

if [[ "${SPECKIT_SKIP_DOC_MODEL_VALIDATE:-0}" = "1" ]]; then
  exit 0
fi

REPO_ROOT="$(git rev-parse --show-toplevel 2>/dev/null || true)"
if [[ -z "$REPO_ROOT" ]]; then
  exit 0
fi

display.text:
#!/usr/bin/env bash
# Pre-commit hook — advisory model-reference drift check.
#
# Runs validate-doc-model-refs.js on the working tree before each commit.
# Advisory mode: prints warnings on drift but does NOT block the commit.
# Bypass: SPECKIT_SKIP_DOC_MODEL_VALIDATE=1 git commit ...
#
# Install: bash .opencode/scripts/install-git-hooks.sh
# Uninstall: rm .git/hooks/pre-commit

set -euo pipefail

if [[ "${SPECKIT_SKIP_DOC_MODEL_VALIDATE:-0}" = "1" ]]; then
  exit 0
fi

REPO_ROOT="$(git rev-parse --show-toplevel 2>/dev/null || true)"
if [[ -z "$REPO_ROOT" ]]; then
  exit 0
fi

VALIDATOR="$REPO_ROOT/.opencode/skills/sk-doc/scripts/validate-doc-model-refs.js"
if [[ ! -f "$VALIDATOR" ]]; then
  exit 0
fi

if ! command -v node >/dev/null 2>&1; then
  exit 0
fi

# Run validator; capture exit code. Non-zero exit means drift detected.
# Advisory: warn on stderr but always exit 0 so the commit proceeds.
RC=0
OUT="$(node "$VALIDATOR" 2>&1)" || RC=$?

if [[ "$RC" -ne 0 ]]; then
  echo "" >&2
  echo "⚠️  doc-model-refs drift detected (advisory; commit not blocked):" >&2
  echo "$OUT" | head -30 >&2 || true
  echo "" >&2
  echo "Bypass next time: SPECKIT_SKIP_DOC_MODEL_VALIDATE=1 git commit ..." >&2
  echo "Fix: update docs to cite canonical model names from registry.ts" >&2
  echo "" >&2
fi

# --- Comment hygiene gate (blocking) ---
# Blocks commits that add ephemeral-artifact pointers in code comment lines.
# Bypass: SPECKIT_SKIP_COMMENT_HYGIENE=1 git commit ...
# See: .opencode/skills/sk-code/references/universal/code_style_guide.md §4
if [[ "${SPECKIT_SKIP_COMMENT_HYGIENE:-0}" != "1" ]]; then
  HYGIENE_HOOK="${REPO_ROOT}/.opencode/hooks/pre-commit"
  if [[ -x "$HYGIENE_HOOK" ]]; then
    bash "$HYGIENE_HOOK" || exit $?
  fi
fi

# --- Prompt-knowledge card-sync gate (blocking) ---
# When a prompt-knowledge surface is staged, run the 3-layer drift guard so
# table inlining, Tier-3 escalation-rule drift, registry/profile/_index gaps
# (zero-hub-weight entries), or models unreachable by name cannot silently
# regress across sk-prompt / sk-prompt-small-model / cli-*.
# Bypass: SPECKIT_SKIP_CARD_SYNC=1 git commit ...
if [[ "${SPECKIT_SKIP_CARD_SYNC:-0}" != "1" ]]; then
  CARD_GUARD="${REPO_ROOT}/.opencode/skills/system-skill-advisor/mcp_server/scripts/check-prompt-quality-card-sync.sh"
  if [[ -f "$CARD_GUARD" ]] && git diff --cached --name-only --diff-filter=ACM \
       | grep -Eq '^\.opencode/skills/(cli-[a-z-]+/(SKILL\.md|assets/prompt_quality_card\.md|graph-metadata\.json)|sk-prompt/assets/cli_prompt_quality_card\.md|sk-prompt-small-model/)'; then
    if ! bash "$CARD_GUARD" "$REPO_ROOT"; then
      echo "" >&2
      echo "BLOCKED: prompt-knowledge drift guard failed (see FAIL lines above)." >&2
      echo "Fix: keep each fact in its one canonical home and point to it; see" >&2
      echo "     .opencode/skills/sk-prompt-small-model/references/pattern_index.md." >&2
      echo "Bypass: SPECKIT_SKIP_CARD_SYNC=1 git commit ..." >&2
      exit 1
    fi
  fi
fi

# --- MCP mutation-class gate (blocking) ---
# When an mcp-* install/doctor script or the install-doctor manifest is staged,
# enforce the read-only / mutating contract: a "read-only" doctor that grows an
# unguarded mutation or unbounded network call (or a missing manifest label)
# fails the commit. Guards against the doctor-that-connected-to-all-servers and
# the installer-that-wiped-a-global-cache regressions.
# Bypass: SPECKIT_SKIP_MCP_MUTATION_CLASS=1 git commit ...
if [[ "${SPECKIT_SKIP_MCP_MUTATION_CLASS:-0}" != "1" ]]; then
  MUTCLASS_GUARD="${REPO_ROOT}/.opencode/commands/doctor/scripts/check-mcp-mutation-class.sh"
  if [[ -f "$MUTCLASS_GUARD" ]] && git diff --cached --name-only --diff-filter=ACM \
       | grep -Eq '^\.opencode/(skills/mcp-[a-z-]+/(scripts/(doctor|install)[a-z.-]*\.sh|mcp-servers/[^/]+/setup\.sh)|commands/doctor/assets/doctor_mcp_install\.yaml)$'; then
    if ! bash "$MUTCLASS_GUARD" "$REPO_ROOT"; then
      echo "" >&2
      echo "BLOCKED: MCP mutation-class contract failed (see FAIL lines above)." >&2
      echo "Fix: a read-only doctor must not run mutations or unbounded network calls;" >&2
      echo "     declare each script's mutation_class in doctor_mcp_install.yaml." >&2
      echo "Bypass: SPECKIT_SKIP_MCP_MUTATION_CLASS=1 git commit ..." >&2
      exit 1
    fi
  fi
fi

# --- Tool ownership map gate (blocking) ---
# Blocks commits when the committed generated map no longer matches TOOL_DEFINITIONS.
# Bypass: SPECKIT_SKIP_TOOL_OWNERSHIP_LINT=1 git commit ...
if [[ "${SPECKIT_SKIP_TOOL_OWNERSHIP_LINT:-0}" != "1" ]]; then
  OWNERSHIP_LINT="${REPO_ROOT}/.opencode/skills/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/tests/tool-ownership-lint-runner.mjs"
  if [[ ! -f "$OWNERSHIP_LINT" ]]; then
    echo "BLOCKED: tool-ownership lint runner missing." >&2
    exit 1
  fi
  if ! node "$OWNERSHIP_LINT" >/dev/null; then
    echo "" >&2
    echo "BLOCKED: tool-ownership drift detected." >&2
    echo "Fix: regenerate mcp_server/tests/fixtures/tool-ownership-map.json from TOOL_DEFINITIONS." >&2
    echo "Bypass: SPECKIT_SKIP_TOOL_OWNERSHIP_LINT=1 git commit ..." >&2
    exit 1
  fi
fi

exit 0

11: // ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

14: 
15: export type LoopType = 'research' | 'review' | 'context';

120: export interface Namespace {

126: // ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
127: // 2. CONSTANTS
128: // ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
129:

131:

134:   dirname(fileURLToPath(import.meta.url)),
135:   '..',

140: const MIN_WEIGHT = 0.0;

143: export const RESEARCH_WEIGHTS: Record<ResearchRelation, number> = {
144:   ANSWERS: 1.3,
145:   SUPPORTS: 1.0,
146:   CONTRADICTS: 0.8,

149:   COVERS: 1.1,
150:   CITES: 1.0,
151: };
152: 
153: export const REVIEW_WEIGHTS: Record<ReviewRelation, number> = {
154:   COVERS: 1.3,
155:   EVIDENCE_FOR: 1.0,
156:   CONTRADICTS: 0.8,
157:   RESOLVES: 1.5,
158:   CONFIRMS: 1.0,
159:   ESCALATES: 1.2,
160:   IN_DIMENSION: 1.0,
161:   IN_FILE: 1.0,
162: };
163: 
164: // Context relation weights: REUSES is highest — surfacing existing code to extend
165: // is the primary value of context gathering (the repo "reuse, avoid new code" principle).
166: export const CONTEXT_WEIGHTS: Record<ContextRelation, number> = {
167:   CONTAINS: 1.0,
168:   REFERENCES: 1.0,
169:   IMPORTS: 1.2,
170:   DEPENDS_ON: 1.2,
171:   IMPLEMENTS: 1.4,
172:   EXPOSES: 1.3,
173:   REUSES: 1.5,
174:   CONSTRAINS: 1.1,
175:   COVERED_BY: 1.1,
176:   CONFIRMS: 1.0,
177:   CONTRADICTS: 0.8,
178: };
179: 
180: export const VALID_KINDS: Record<LoopType, readonly string[]> = {
181:   research: ['QUESTION', 'FINDING', 'CLAIM', 'SOURCE'] as const,
182:   review: [
183:     'DIMENSION',
184:     'FILE',
185:     'FINDING',
186:     'EVIDENCE',
187:     'REMEDIATION',
188:     'BUG_CLASS',
189:     'INVARIANT',
190:     'PRODUCER',
191:     'CONSUMER',
192:     'TEST',
193:   ] as const,
194:   context: [
195:     'SLICE',
196:     'FILE',
197:     'SYMBOL',
198:     'PATTERN',
199:     'REUSE_CANDIDATE',
200:     'DEPENDENCY',
201:     'CONSTRAINT',
202:     'GAP',
203:   ] as const,
204: };
205: 
206: export const VALID_RELATIONS: Record<LoopType, readonly string[]> = {
207:   research: ['ANSWERS', 'SUPPORTS', 'CONTRADICTS', 'SUPERSEDES', 'DERIVED_FROM', 'COVERS', 'CITES'] as const,
208:   review: ['COVERS', 'EVIDENCE_FOR', 'CONTRADICTS', 'RESOLVES', 'CONFIRMS', 'ESCALATES', 'IN_DIMENSION', 'IN_FILE'] as const,
209:   context: ['CONTAINS', 'REFERENCES', 'IMPORTS', 'DEPENDS_ON', 'IMPLEMENTS', 'EXPOSES', 'REUSES', 'CONSTRAINS', 'COVERED_BY', 'CONFIRMS', 'CONTRADICTS'] as const,
210: };
211: 
212: // ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
213: // 3. SCHEMA
214: // ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
215: 
216: const SCHEMA_SQL = `
217:   CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS coverage_nodes (
218:     spec_folder TEXT NOT NULL,
219:     loop_type TEXT NOT NULL CHECK(loop_type IN ('research', 'review', 'context')),
220:     session_id TEXT NOT NULL,
221:     id TEXT NOT NULL,
222:     kind TEXT NOT NULL,
223:     name TEXT NOT NULL,
224:     content_hash TEXT,
225:     iteration INTEGER,
226:     metadata TEXT,
227:     created_at TEXT DEFAULT (datetime('now')),
228:     updated_at TEXT DEFAULT (datetime('now')),
229:     PRIMARY KEY (spec_folder, loop_type, session_id, id)
230:   );
231: 
232:   CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS coverage_edges (
233:     spec_folder TEXT NOT NULL,
234:     loop_type TEXT NOT NULL,
235:     session_id TEXT NOT NULL,
236:     id TEXT NOT NULL,
237:     source_id TEXT NOT NULL,
238:     target_id TEXT NOT NULL,
239:     relation TEXT NOT NULL,
240:     weight REAL DEFAULT 1.0 CHECK(weight >= 0.0 AND weight <= 2.0),
241:     metadata TEXT,
242:     created_at TEXT DEFAULT (datetime('now')),
243:     CHECK(source_id != target_id),
244:     PRIMARY KEY (spec_folder, loop_type, session_id, id),
245:     FOREIGN KEY (spec_folder, loop_type, session_id, source_id)
246:       REFERENCES coverage_nodes (spec_folder, loop_type, session_id, id),
247:     FOREIGN KEY (spec_folder, loop_type, session_id, target_id)
248:       REFERENCES coverage_nodes (spec_folder, loop_type, session_id, id)
249:   );
250: 
251:   CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS coverage_snapshots (
252:     id INTEGER PRIMARY KEY AUTOINCREMENT,
253:     spec_folder TEXT NOT NULL,
254:     loop_type TEXT NOT NULL,
255:     session_id TEXT NOT NULL,
256:     iteration INTEGER NOT NULL,
257:     metrics TEXT,
258:     node_count INTEGER,
259:     edge_count INTEGER,
260:     created_at TEXT DEFAULT (datetime('now')),
261:     UNIQUE(spec_folder, loop_type, session_id, iteration)
262:   );
263: 
264:   CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS schema_version (
265:     version INTEGER NOT NULL
266:   );
267: 
268:   CREATE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS idx_coverage_folder_type ON coverage_nodes(spec_folder, loop_type);
269:   CREATE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS idx_coverage_kind ON coverage_nodes(kind);
270:   CREATE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS idx_coverage_session ON coverage_nodes(spec_folder, loop_type, session_id);
271:   CREATE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS idx_coverage_iteration ON coverage_nodes(iteration);
272:   CREATE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS idx_coverage_edge_source ON coverage_edges(spec_folder, loop_type, session_id, source_id);
273:   CREATE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS idx_coverage_edge_target ON coverage_edges(spec_folder, loop_type, session_id, target_id);
274:   CREATE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS idx_coverage_edge_relation ON coverage_edges(relation);
275:   CREATE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS idx_coverage_edge_folder_type ON coverage_edges(spec_folder, loop_type);
276:   CREATE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS idx_coverage_edge_session ON coverage_edges(spec_folder, loop_type, session_id);
277:   CREATE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS idx_coverage_snapshot_session ON coverage_snapshots(session_id);
278: `;
279: 
280: // ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
281: // 4. DATABASE LIFECYCLE
282: // ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
283: 
284: let db: Database.Database | null = null;
285: let dbPath: string | null = null;
286: let statementDb: Database.Database | null = null;
287: const preparedStatements = new Map<string, Database.Statement>();
288: 
289: /**
290:  * Initialize (or get) the coverage graph database.
291:  *
292:  * Creates the database directory and file, applies schema migrations,
293:  * and enables WAL journaling and foreign keys.
294:  *
295:  * @param dbDir - Directory for the database file.
296:  * @returns The initialized Database instance.
297:  */
298: export function initDb(dbDir: string): Database.Database {
299:   if (db) return db;
300: 
301:   try {
302:     mkdirSync(dbDir, { recursive: true });
303:     dbPath = join(dbDir, DB_FILENAME);
304:     db = new Database(dbPath);
305:     db.pragma('journal_mode = WAL');
306:     db.pragma('foreign_keys = ON');
307: 
308:     const schemaTableExists = db.prepare(
309:       "SELECT name FROM sqlite_master WHERE type='table' AND name='schema_version'",
310:     ).get() as { name: string } | undefined;
311:     if (schemaTableExists) {
312:       const existing = db.prepare('SELECT version FROM schema_version LIMIT 1').get() as { version: number } | undefined;
313:       if (existing && existing.version < SCHEMA_VERSION) {
314:         db.exec(`
315:           DROP INDEX IF EXISTS idx_coverage_folder_type;
316:           DROP INDEX IF EXISTS idx_coverage_kind;
317:           DROP INDEX IF EXISTS idx_coverage_session;
318:           DROP INDEX IF EXISTS idx_coverage_iteration;
319:           DROP INDEX IF EXISTS idx_coverage_edge_source;
320:           DROP INDEX IF EXISTS idx_coverage_edge_target;
321:           DROP INDEX IF EXISTS idx_coverage_edge_relation;
322:           DROP INDEX IF EXISTS idx_coverage_edge_folder_type;
323:           DROP INDEX IF EXISTS idx_coverage_edge_session;
324:           DROP INDEX IF EXISTS idx_coverage_snapshot_session;
325:           DROP TABLE IF EXISTS coverage_edges;
326:           DROP TABLE IF EXISTS coverage_nodes;
327:           DROP TABLE IF EXISTS coverage_snapshots;
328:         `);
329:       }
330:     }
331: 
332:     db.exec(SCHEMA_SQL);
333: 
334:     const versionRow = db.prepare('SELECT version FROM schema_version LIMIT 1').get() as { version: number } | undefined;
335:     if (!versionRow) {
336:       db.prepare('INSERT INTO schema_version (version) VALUES (?)').run(SCHEMA_VERSION);
337:     } else if (versionRow.version < SCHEMA_VERSION) {
338:       db.prepare('UPDATE schema_version SET version = ?').run(SCHEMA_VERSION);
339:     }
340: 
341:     return db;
342:   } catch (err: unknown) {
343:     if (db) {
344:       try { db.close(); } catch { }
345:     }
346:     db = null;
347:     dbPath = null;
348:     throw err;
349:   }
350: }
351: 
352: /**
353:  * Get the current database instance (lazy-initializes if needed).
354:  *
355:  * @returns The Database instance.
356:  */
357: export function getDb(): Database.Database {
358:   if (!db) initDb(COVERAGE_GRAPH_DATABASE_DIR);
359:   return db!;
360: }
361: 
362: /**
363:  * Close the database connection and clear cached state.
364:  */
365: export function closeDb(): void {
366:   if (db) {
367:     db.close();
368:     db = null;
369:     dbPath = null;
370:     statementDb = null;
371:     preparedStatements.clear();
372:   }
373: }
374: 
375: // ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
376: // 5. HELPERS
377: // ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
378: 
379: function prepareStatement(sql: string): Database.Statement {
380:   const currentDb = getDb();
381:   if (statementDb !== currentDb) {
382:     preparedStatements.clear();
383:     statementDb = currentDb;
384:   }
385: 
386:   let statement = preparedStatements.get(sql);
387:   if (!statement) {
388:     statement = currentDb.prepare(sql);
389:     preparedStatements.set(sql, statement);
390:   }
391:   return statement;
392: }
393: 
394: function buildNamespaceWhere(ns: Namespace): { clause: string; params: unknown[] } {
395:   const parts: string[] = ['spec_folder = ?', 'loop_type = ?'];
396:   const params: unknown[] = [ns.specFolder, ns.loopType];
397:   if (ns.sessionId) {
398:     parts.push('session_id = ?');
399:     params.push(ns.sessionId);
400:   }
401:   return { clause: parts.join(' AND '), params };
402: }
403: 
404: function rowToNode(r: Record<string, unknown>): CoverageNode {
405:   return {
406:     id: r.id as string,
407:     specFolder: r.spec_folder as string,
408:     loopType: r.loop_type as LoopType,
409:     sessionId: r.session_id as string,
410:     kind: r.kind as NodeKind,
411:     name: r.name as string,
412:     contentHash: r.content_hash as string | undefined,
413:     iteration: r.iteration as number | undefined,
414:     metadata: r.metadata ? JSON.parse(r.metadata as string) : undefined,
415:     createdAt: r.created_at as string | undefined,
416:     updatedAt: r.updated_at as string | undefined,
417:   };
418: }
419: 
420: function rowToEdge(r: Record<string, unknown>): CoverageEdge {
421:   return {
422:     id: r.id as string,
423:     specFolder: r.spec_folder as string,
424:     loopType: r.loop_type as LoopType,
425:     sessionId: r.session_id as string,
426:     sourceId: r.source_id as string,
427:     targetId: r.target_id as string,
428:     relation: r.relation as Relation,
429:     weight: r.weight as number,
430:     metadata: r.metadata ? JSON.parse(r.metadata as string) : undefined,
431:     createdAt: r.created_at as string | undefined,
432:   };
433: }
434: 
435: function rowToSnapshot(r: Record<string, unknown>): CoverageSnapshot {
436:   return {
437:     id: r.id as number,
438:     specFolder: r.spec_folder as string,
439:     loopType: r.loop_type as LoopType,
440:     sessionId: r.session_id as string,
441:     iteration: r.iteration as number,
442:     metrics: r.metrics ? JSON.parse(r.metrics as string) : {},
443:     nodeCount: r.node_count as number,
444:     edgeCount: r.edge_count as number,
445:     createdAt: r.created_at as string | undefined,
446:   };

1: // ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
2: // MODULE: Deep-Loop Executor Config
3: // ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
4: 
5: import { z } from 'zod';
6: 
7: // ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
8: // 1. TYPE DEFINITIONS
9: // ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
10: 
11: export const EXECUTOR_KINDS = ['native', 'cli-codex', 'cli-claude-code', 'cli-opencode'] as const;
12: export type ExecutorKind = typeof EXECUTOR_KINDS[number];
13: 
14: export const REASONING_EFFORTS = ['none', 'minimal', 'low', 'medium', 'high', 'xhigh'] as const;
15: export type ReasoningEffort = typeof REASONING_EFFORTS[number];
16: 
17: export const SERVICE_TIERS = ['priority', 'standard', 'fast'] as const;
18: export type ServiceTier = typeof SERVICE_TIERS[number];
19: 
20: const SANDBOX_MODES = ['read-only', 'workspace-write', 'danger-full-access'] as const;
21: export type SandboxMode = typeof SANDBOX_MODES[number];
22: export type ClaudePermissionMode = 'plan' | 'acceptEdits' | 'bypassPermissions';
23: 
24: // ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
25: // 2. CONSTANTS
26: // ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
27: 
28: export const executorConfigSchema = z.object({
29:   kind: z.enum(EXECUTOR_KINDS).default('native'),
30:   model: z.string().min(1).nullable().default(null),
31:   configDir: z.string().trim().min(1).nullable().default(null),
32:   reasoningEffort: z.enum(REASONING_EFFORTS).nullable().default(null),
33:   serviceTier: z.enum(SERVICE_TIERS).nullable().default(null),
34:   sandboxMode: z.enum(SANDBOX_MODES).nullable().default(null),
35:   timeoutSeconds: z.number().int().positive().default(900),
36:   // Optional fable-5 governor capsule for this executor's prompts. Universal and
37:   // kind-agnostic on purpose: intentionally absent from EXECUTOR_KIND_FLAG_SUPPORT
38:   // and the unsupported-field scan, so any executor kind may carry it. null = none.
39:   governor: z.string().min(1).nullable().default(null),
40: });
41: 
42: export type ExecutorConfig = z.infer<typeof executorConfigSchema>;
43: 
44: export const EXECUTOR_KIND_FLAG_SUPPORT: Record<ExecutorKind, readonly (keyof ExecutorConfig)[]> = {
45:   native: [],
46:   'cli-codex': ['model', 'reasoningEffort', 'serviceTier', 'sandboxMode', 'timeoutSeconds'],
47:   'cli-claude-code': ['model', 'configDir', 'reasoningEffort', 'sandboxMode', 'timeoutSeconds'],
48:   'cli-opencode': ['model', 'reasoningEffort', 'timeoutSeconds'],
49: };
50: 
51: // ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
52: // 3. DOMAIN ERRORS
53: // ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
54: 
55: type ExecutorConfigIssue = {
56:   path: PropertyKey[];
57:   message: string;
58: };
59: 
60: /** Error thrown when a reserved executor kind is selected before wiring exists. */
61: export class ExecutorNotWiredError extends Error {
62:   kind: Extract<ExecutorKind, 'cli-claude-code'>;
63: 
64:   constructor(kind: Extract<ExecutorKind, 'cli-claude-code'>) {
65:     super(`Executor kind '${kind}' is reserved in the schema but not yet wired. Awaiting future spec for ${kind} integration.`);
66:     this.name = 'ExecutorNotWiredError';
67:     this.kind = kind;
68:   }
69: }
70: 
71: /** Error thrown when executor configuration validation fails. */
72: export class ExecutorConfigError extends Error {
73:   issues: ExecutorConfigIssue[];
74: 
75:   constructor(input: { issues: ExecutorConfigIssue[] }) {
76:     super(input.issues.map((issue) => `${issue.path.map(String).join('.') || '<root>'}: ${issue.message}`).join('; '));
77:     this.name = 'ExecutorConfigError';
78:     this.issues = input.issues;
79:   }
80: }
81: 
82: // ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
83: // 4. HELPERS
84: // ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
85: 
86: function isRecord(value: unknown): value is Record<string, unknown> {
87:   return value !== null && !Array.isArray(value) && typeof value === 'object';
88: }
89: 
90: function normalizeSandboxMode(mode: SandboxMode | null | undefined): SandboxMode {
91:   return mode ?? 'workspace-write';
92: }
93: 
94: function normalizeExecutorConfigInput(raw: unknown): unknown {
95:   if (!isRecord(raw) || !Object.prototype.hasOwnProperty.call(raw, 'type')) {
96:     return raw;
97:   }
98: 
99:   const legacyKind = raw.type;
100:   if (typeof legacyKind !== 'string') {
101:     return raw;
102:   }
103: 
104:   if (typeof raw.kind === 'string' && raw.kind !== legacyKind) {
105:     throw new ExecutorConfigError({
106:       issues: [{
107:         path: ['type'],
108:         message: `deprecated executor field 'type' conflicts with canonical kind '${raw.kind}'`,
109:       }],
110:     });
111:   }
112: 
113:   console.warn("[executor-config] Deprecated executor field 'type' was provided; use 'kind' instead.");
114:   const { type: _legacyType, ...rest } = raw;
115:   return {
116:     ...rest,
117:     kind: typeof raw.kind === 'string' ? raw.kind : legacyKind,
118:   };
119: }
120: 
121: function normalizeIssues(error: z.ZodError<ExecutorConfig>): ExecutorConfigIssue[] {
122:   return error.issues.map((issue) => ({
123:     path: issue.path,
124:     message: issue.message,
125:   }));
126: }
127: 
128: // ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
129: // 5. EXPORTS
130: // ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
131: 
132: /**
133:  * Map a generic sandbox mode to the Codex CLI sandbox mode.
134:  *
135:  * @param mode - Generic sandbox mode or undefined.
136:  * @returns Codex-compatible sandbox mode.
137:  */
138: export function resolveCodexSandboxMode(mode: SandboxMode | null | undefined): SandboxMode {
139:   return normalizeSandboxMode(mode);
140: }
141: 
142: /**
143:  * Map a generic sandbox mode to the Claude Code permission mode.
144:  *
145:  * @param mode - Generic sandbox mode or undefined.
146:  * @returns Claude-compatible permission mode.
147:  */
148: export function resolveClaudePermissionMode(mode: SandboxMode | null | undefined): ClaudePermissionMode {
149:   switch (normalizeSandboxMode(mode)) {
150:     case 'read-only':
151:       return 'plan';
152:     case 'danger-full-access':
153:       return 'bypassPermissions';
154:     default:
155:       return 'acceptEdits';
156:   }
157: }
158: 
159: /**
160:  * Parse and validate a raw executor configuration.
161:  *
162:  * Normalizes legacy `type` fields, validates against the Zod schema,
163:  * and enforces executor-kind-specific field support.
164:  *
165:  * @param raw - Raw input to parse (JSON-parsed object).
166:  * @returns Validated ExecutorConfig.
167:  * @throws {@link ExecutorConfigError} If validation fails.
168:  */
169: export function parseExecutorConfig(raw: unknown): ExecutorConfig {
170:   const parsed = executorConfigSchema.safeParse(normalizeExecutorConfigInput(raw));
171:   if (!parsed.success) {
172:     throw new ExecutorConfigError({
173:       issues: normalizeIssues(parsed.error),
174:     });
175:   }
176: 
177:   const config = parsed.data;
178:   if (config.kind === 'cli-codex' && config.model === null) {
179:     throw new ExecutorConfigError({
180:       issues: [{ path: ['model'], message: 'model is required when kind is cli-codex' }],
181:     });
182:   }
183:   const supportedFlags = EXECUTOR_KIND_FLAG_SUPPORT[config.kind];
184:   const unsupportedFields: string[] = [];
185:   const allOptionalFields: (keyof ExecutorConfig)[] = [
186:     'model',
187:     'configDir',
188:     'reasoningEffort',
189:     'serviceTier',
190:     'sandboxMode',
191:     'timeoutSeconds',
192:   ];
193: 
194:   for (const field of allOptionalFields) {
195:     if (!supportedFlags.includes(field) && config[field] !== null && !(field === 'timeoutSeconds' && config[field] === 900)) {
196:       unsupportedFields.push(field);
197:     }
198:   }
199: 
200:   if (unsupportedFields.length > 0) {
201:     throw new ExecutorConfigError({
202:       issues: unsupportedFields.map((field) => ({
203:         path: [field],
204:         message: `field '${field}' is not supported by executor kind '${config.kind}'. Supported fields for ${config.kind}: ${
205:           supportedFlags.length ? supportedFlags.join(', ') : 'none'
206:         }.`,
207:       })),
208:     });
209:   }
210: 
211:   return config;
212: }
213: 
214: /**
215:  * Resolve an executor configuration by merging CLI and file sources.
216:  *
217:  * @param sources - CLI and file partial configs (CLI takes precedence).
218:  * @returns Validated ExecutorConfig.
219:  */
220: export function resolveExecutorConfig(sources: {
221:   cli?: Partial<ExecutorConfig>;
222:   file?: Partial<ExecutorConfig>;
223: }): ExecutorConfig {
224:   const merged = {
225:     ...(sources.file ?? {}),
226:     ...(sources.cli ?? {}),
227:   };
228:   return parseExecutorConfig(merged);
229: }
230: 
231: // ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
232: // 6. FAN-OUT CONFIG
233: // ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
234: //
235: // Opt-in layer ABOVE the single-executor path. When a fan-out config is present,
236: // the loop runs N executor "lineages" concurrently (capped), each running the
237: // existing sequential loop in its own isolated sub-packet. The single-executor
238: // path (executorConfigSchema / parseExecutorConfig) is untouched and remains the
239: // default; callers use EITHER a single `executor` OR a `fanout` block, never both.
240: 
241: const LINEAGE_LABEL_PATTERN = /^[a-z0-9][a-z0-9-]*$/;
242: 
243: /**
244:  * One fan-out lineage: a single executor config plus fan-out metadata.
245:  *
246:  * - `label`: directory-safe id for the lineage's isolated sub-packet.
247:  * - `count`: number of replicas of THIS config to run (e.g. 5x the same model).
248:  * - `iterations`: per-lineage max-iterations override; null = packet default.
249:  * - `promptFramework`: optional per-model prompt framing key (e.g. "costar",
250:  *   "tidd-ec") resolved against sk-prompt-small-model when a consumer renders the
251:  *   lineage prompt. null = consumer default. Loop-type-agnostic and optional, so
252:  *   research/review lineages that omit it are unaffected.
253:  */
254: export const lineageExecutorSchema = executorConfigSchema.extend({
255:   label: z.string().min(1).regex(LINEAGE_LABEL_PATTERN, {
256:     message: "label must match /^[a-z0-9][a-z0-9-]*$/ (lowercase, digits, hyphens; dir-safe)",
257:   }),
258:   count: z.number().int().positive().default(1),
259:   iterations: z.number().int().positive().nullable().default(null),
260:   promptFramework: z.string().min(1).nullable().default(null),
261: });
262: 
263: export type LineageExecutor = z.infer<typeof lineageExecutorSchema>;
264: 
265: export const fanoutConfigSchema = z.object({
266:   executors: z.array(lineageExecutorSchema).min(1),
267:   concurrency: z.number().int().positive().default(2),
268: });
269: 
270: export type FanoutConfig = z.infer<typeof fanoutConfigSchema>;
271: 
272: /**
273:  * Parse and validate a raw fan-out configuration.
274:  *
275:  * Each entry's executor subset is routed through {@link parseExecutorConfig} so
276:  * ALL existing kind/model/flag rules (cli-codex needs model, per-kind flag
277:  * support) apply per lineage with zero duplication.
278:  * Labels must be unique and base-label (count>1) expansion must not collide.
279:  *
280:  * @param raw - Raw input to parse (JSON-parsed object).
281:  * @returns Validated FanoutConfig.
282:  * @throws {@link ExecutorConfigError} If validation fails.
283:  */
284: export function parseFanoutConfig(raw: unknown): FanoutConfig {
285:   const parsed = fanoutConfigSchema.safeParse(raw);
286:   if (!parsed.success) {
287:     throw new ExecutorConfigError({ issues: parsed.error.issues.map((issue) => ({ path: issue.path, message: issue.message })) });
288:   }
289: 
290:   const config = parsed.data;
291: 
292:   // Reuse the canonical single-executor validator per entry (kind/model/flags).
293:   config.executors.forEach((entry, index) => {
294:     const { label: _label, count: _count, iterations: _iterations, promptFramework: _promptFramework, ...executorSubset } = entry;
295:     try {
296:       parseExecutorConfig(executorSubset);
297:     } catch (err: unknown) {
298:       if (err instanceof ExecutorConfigError) {
299:         throw new ExecutorConfigError({
300:           issues: err.issues.map((issue) => ({ path: ['executors', index, ...issue.path], message: issue.message })),
301:         });
302:       }
303:       throw err;
304:     }
305:   });
306: 
307:   // Labels must be unique among the declared lineages.
308:   const seenLabels = new Set<string>();
309:   config.executors.forEach((entry, index) => {
310:     if (seenLabels.has(entry.label)) {
311:       throw new ExecutorConfigError({
312:         issues: [{ path: ['executors', index, 'label'], message: `duplicate lineage label '${entry.label}'` }],
313:       });
314:     }
315:     seenLabels.add(entry.label);
316:   });
317: 
318:   // Expanded labels (count>1 → label-1, label-2, …) must not collide either.
319:   const expandedLabels = new Set<string>();
320:   for (const lineage of expandLineages(config)) {
321:     if (expandedLabels.has(lineage.label)) {
322:       throw new ExecutorConfigError({
323:         issues: [{ path: ['executors'], message: `expanded lineage label '${lineage.label}' collides; rename base labels` }],
324:       });
325:     }
326:     expandedLabels.add(lineage.label);
327:   }
328: 
329:   return config;
330: }
331: 
332: /**
333:  * Expand a fan-out config into concrete per-replica lineages.
334:  *
335:  * A `count` of 1 keeps the base label; `count > 1` yields `${label}-1` …
336:  * `${label}-N`. Each expanded lineage carries `count: 1` so downstream
337:  * consumers see one runnable unit per element.
338:  *
339:  * @param config - Validated FanoutConfig.
340:  * @returns Flat list of single-replica lineages (one runnable unit each).
341:  */
342: export function expandLineages(config: FanoutConfig): LineageExecutor[] {
343:   const lineages: LineageExecutor[] = [];
344:   for (const entry of config.executors) {
345:     if (entry.count === 1) {
346:       lineages.push({ ...entry, count: 1 });
347:       continue;
348:     }
349:     for (let replica = 1; replica <= entry.count; replica += 1) {
350:       lineages.push({ ...entry, label: `${entry.label}-${replica}`, count: 1 });

1: // ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
2: // MODULE: Deep-Loop Atomic State
3: // ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
4: 
5: import { closeSync, existsSync, fsyncSync, openSync, renameSync, rmSync, writeFileSync } from 'node:fs';
6: import { dirname } from 'node:path';
7: 
8: // ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
9: // 1. HELPERS
10: // ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
11: 
12: function fsyncPath(path: string): void {
13:   let fd: number | undefined;
14:   try {
15:     fd = openSync(path, 'r');
16:     fsyncSync(fd);
17:   } finally {
18:     if (typeof fd === 'number') {
19:       closeSync(fd);
20:     }
21:   }
22: }
23: 
24: function makeTempPath(targetPath: string): string {
25:   return `${targetPath}.tmp.${process.pid}.${Date.now()}.${Math.random().toString(36).slice(2)}`;
26: }
27: 
28: // ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
29: // 2. EXPORTS
30: // ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
31: 
32: /**
33:  * Atomically write JSON-serializable state to a file.
34:  *
35:  * Writes to a temp file first, fsyncs it, then renames onto the target
36:  * so readers always see either the previous complete file or the new one.
37:  *
38:  * @param path - Target file path.
39:  * @param data - Serializable data to write.
40:  * @throws If write, fsync, or rename fails.
41:  */
42: export function writeStateAtomic(path: string, data: unknown): void {
43:   writeTextAtomic(path, `${JSON.stringify(data, null, 2)}\n`);
44: }
45: 
46: /**
47:  * Atomically write a text payload to a file.
48:  *
49:  * Same temp + fsync + rename guarantee as writeStateAtomic, but for
50:  * already-serialized content (e.g. markdown) that must not be re-encoded
51:  * as JSON. Readers always see either the previous complete file or the
52:  * new one, never a torn mid-write tail.
53:  *
54:  * @param path - Target file path.
55:  * @param content - Exact bytes to write.
56:  * @throws If write, fsync, or rename fails.
57:  */
58: export function writeTextAtomic(path: string, content: string): void {
59:   const tempPath = makeTempPath(path);
60: 
61:   try {
62:     writeFileSync(tempPath, content, 'utf8');
63:     fsyncPath(tempPath);
64:     renameSync(tempPath, path);
65:     try {
66:       fsyncPath(dirname(path));
67:     } catch {
68:     }
69:   } catch (error: unknown) {
70:     if (existsSync(tempPath)) {
71:       rmSync(tempPath, { force: true });
72:     }
73:     throw error;
74:   }
75: }

# function definitions (e.g. a unit test exercising has_live_ipc_socket_connection),
# so skip the process-scanning/kill main flow.
(return 0 2>/dev/null) && return 0

# ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

rotate_log_if_needed
emit "action=start dry_run=$DRY_RUN verbose=$VERBOSE age_min_sec=$AGE_MIN_SEC tmp_age_hours=$TMP_AGE_HOURS"
build_session_trees
scan_processes
select_kill_candidates

metadata.preview:
remember_min_for_class() {
  local class="$1"
  local age="$2"
  local pid="$3"
  local idx
  idx="$(class_index "$class" 2>/dev/null || true)"
  if [[ -z "$idx" ]]; then
    MIN_CLASSES+=("$class")
    MIN_AGES+=("$age")
    MIN_PIDS+=("$pid")
    return
  fi
  if (( age < MIN_AGES[idx] )); then
    MIN_AGES[idx]="$age"
    MIN_PIDS[idx]="$pid"
  fi
}

display.text:
remember_min_for_class() {
  local class="$1"
  local age="$2"
  local pid="$3"
  local idx
  idx="$(class_index "$class" 2>/dev/null || true)"
  if [[ -z "$idx" ]]; then
    MIN_CLASSES+=("$class")
    MIN_AGES+=("$age")
    MIN_PIDS+=("$pid")
    return
  fi
  if (( age < MIN_AGES[idx] )); then
    MIN_AGES[idx]="$age"
    MIN_PIDS[idx]="$pid"
  fi
}

diff --git a/.opencode/scripts/git-hooks/pre-commit b/.opencode/scripts/git-hooks/pre-commit
index 3585f6818f..635d57f581 100755
--- a/.opencode/scripts/git-hooks/pre-commit
+++ b/.opencode/scripts/git-hooks/pre-commit
@@ -8,19 +8,19 @@
 # Install: bash .opencode/scripts/install-git-hooks.sh
 # Uninstall: rm .git/hooks/pre-commit
 
-set -u
+set -euo pipefail
 
-if [ "${SPECKIT_SKIP_DOC_MODEL_VALIDATE:-0}" = "1" ]; then
+if [[ "${SPECKIT_SKIP_DOC_MODEL_VALIDATE:-0}" = "1" ]]; then
   exit 0
 fi
 
-REPO_ROOT="$(git rev-parse --show-toplevel 2>/dev/null)"
-if [ -z "$REPO_ROOT" ]; then
+REPO_ROOT="$(git rev-parse --show-toplevel 2>/dev/null || true)"
+if [[ -z "$REPO_ROOT" ]]; then
   exit 0
 fi
 
 VALIDATOR="$REPO_ROOT/.opencode/skills/sk-doc/scripts/validate-doc-model-refs.js"
-if [ ! -f "$VALIDATOR" ]; then
+if [[ ! -f "$VALIDATOR" ]]; then
   exit 0
 fi
 
@@ -30,13 +30,13 @@ fi
 
 # Run validator; capture exit code. Non-zero exit means drift detected.
 # Advisory: warn on stderr but always exit 0 so the commit proceeds.
-OUT="$(node "$VALIDATOR" 2>&1)"
-RC=$?
+RC=0
+OUT="$(node "$VALIDATOR" 2>&1)" || RC=$?
 
-if [ "$RC" -ne 0 ]; then
+if [[ "$RC" -ne 0 ]]; then
   echo "" >&2
   echo "⚠️  doc-model-refs drift detected (advisory; commit not blocked):" >&2
-  echo "$OUT" | head -30 >&2
+  echo "$OUT" | head -30 >&2 || true
   echo "" >&2
   echo "Bypass next time: SPECKIT_SKIP_DOC_MODEL_VALIDATE=1 git commit ..." >&2
   echo "Fix: update docs to cite canonical model names from registry.ts" >&2
@@ -47,9 +47,9 @@ fi
 # Blocks commits that add ephemeral-artifact pointers in code comment lines.
 # Bypass: SPECKIT_SKIP_COMMENT_HYGIENE=1 git commit ...
 # See: .opencode/skills/sk-code/references/universal/code_style_guide.md §4
-if [ "${SPECKIT_SKIP_COMMENT_HYGIENE:-0}" != "1" ]; then
+if [[ "${SPECKIT_SKIP_COMMENT_HYGIENE:-0}" != "1" ]]; then
   HYGIENE_HOOK="${REPO_ROOT}/.opencode/hooks/pre-commit"
-  if [ -x "$HYGIENE_HOOK" ]; then
+  if [[ -x "$HYGIENE_HOOK" ]]; then
     bash "$HYGIENE_HOOK" || exit $?
   fi
 fi
@@ -60,9 +60,9 @@ fi
 # (zero-hub-weight entries), or models unreachable by name cannot silently
 # regress across sk-prompt / sk-prompt-small-model / cli-*.
 # Bypass: SPECKIT_SKIP_CARD_SYNC=1 git commit ...
-if [ "${SPECKIT_SKIP_CARD_SYNC:-0}" != "1" ]; then
+if [[ "${SPECKIT_SKIP_CARD_SYNC:-0}" != "1" ]]; then
   CARD_GUARD="${REPO_ROOT}/.opencode/skills/system-skill-advisor/mcp_server/scripts/check-prompt-quality-card-sync.sh"
-  if [ -f "$CARD_GUARD" ] && git diff --cached --name-only --diff-filter=ACM \
+  if [[ -f "$CARD_GUARD" ]] && git diff --cached --name-only --diff-filter=ACM \
        | grep -Eq '^\.opencode/skills/(cli-[a-z-]+/(SKILL\.md|assets/prompt_quality_card\.md|graph-metadata\.json)|sk-prompt/assets/cli_prompt_quality_card\.md|sk-prompt-small-model/)'; then
     if ! bash "$CARD_GUARD" "$REPO_ROOT"; then
       echo "" >&2
@@ -82,9 +82,9 @@ fi
 # fails the commit. Guards against the doctor-that-connected-to-all-servers and
 # the installer-that-wiped-a-global-cache regressions.
 # Bypass: SPECKIT_SKIP_MCP_MUTATION_CLASS=1 git commit ...
-if [ "${SPECKIT_SKIP_MCP_MUTATION_CLASS:-0}" != "1" ]; then
+if [[ "${SPECKIT_SKIP_MCP_MUTATION_CLASS:-0}" != "1" ]]; then
   MUTCLASS_GUARD="${REPO_ROOT}/.opencode/commands/doctor/scripts/check-mcp-mutation-class.sh"
-  if [ -f "$MUTCLASS_GUARD" ] && git diff --cached --name-only --diff-filter=ACM \
+  if [[ -f "$MUTCLASS_GUARD" ]] && git diff --cached --name-only --diff-filter=ACM \
        | grep -Eq '^\.opencode/(skills/mcp-[a-z-]+/(scripts/(doctor|install)[a-z.-]*\.sh|mcp-servers/[^/]+/setup\.sh)|commands/doctor/assets/doctor_mcp_install\.yaml)$'; then
     if ! bash "$MUTCLASS_GUARD" "$REPO_ROOT"; then
       echo "" >&2
@@ -100,9 +100,9 @@ fi
 # --- Tool ownership map gate (blocking) ---
 # Blocks commits when the committed generated map no longer matches TOOL_DEFINITIONS.
 # Bypass: SPECKIT_SKIP_TOOL_OWNERSHIP_LINT=1 git commit ...
-if [ "${SPECKIT_SKIP_TOOL_OWNERSHIP_LINT:-0}" != "1" ]; then

-  if [ ! -f "$OWNERSHIP_LINT" ]; then
